article_title: AMD Begins Production Ramp of EPYC Venice on TSMC 2nm, First HPC Chip on Advanced Node
summary: Solid semiconductor article; one Verano quote paraphrases the AMD press release inside quote marks

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: AMD Begins Production Ramp of EPYC Venice on TSMC 2nm, First HPC Chip on Advanced Node
content_preview.summary: AMD's 6th Gen EPYC 'Venice' is ramping on TSMC 2nm — the first HPC chip on the node — with 256 Zen 6 cores and 1.6 TB/s memory bandwidth.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

AMD announced on May 21, 2026 that its sixth-generation EPYC server processor, codenamed "Venice," has entered production ramp on TSMC's 2nm process technology in Taiwan — making it the first high-performance computing chip in the industry to reach volume manufacturing on the advanced node, according to the company's [official press release on GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/21/3299003/0/en/AMD-Announces-Production-Ramp-of-Next-Generation-AMD-EPYC-P...

recommendations: File corrections record for the paraphrased Verano quote.
Consider adding tweaktown.com and dlcompare.com to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured semiconductor news article (722 words). Good technical depth covering specifications, packaging technology, executive quotes, and competitive context. Internal link to AMD EPYC market share article verified.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (GlobeNewswire/AMD press release): confirmed 'Venice' as first HPC product on TSMC 2nm, Lisa Su and C.C. Wei quotes verbatim, SoIC-X and CoWoS-L packaging, Taiwan production with Arizona plans. Verano quote in article ('being optimized for performance per dollar per watt and will include native LPDDR support to meet the memory demands of agentic AI workloads') is a paraphrase — press release says 'optimized for performance-per-dollar-per-watt leadership' with LPDDR support, but not verbatim as quoted. source-1.html.gz (StockTitan): confirmed Lisa Su quotes. source-2.html.gz (Tweaktown): confirmed 256 cores (33% over Turin's 192), 70% performance improvement, 'first HPC product in the entire industry' claim. source-3.html.gz (Tom's Hardware via Archive.org): confirmed TSMC N2 volume production late 2025, five 2nm fabs ramping 2026, Intel P-core Xeon not expected until 2027. source-4.html.gz (DLCompare): confirmed 1.6 TB/s bandwidth vs 614 GB/s on Turin, 2x CPU-to-GPU bandwidth, Verano LPDDR support.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical specifications verified. One paraphrase in quote marks found: the Verano quote attributed to the AMD press release is not verbatim — the article says 'being optimized for performance per dollar per watt and will include native LPDDR support to meet the memory demands of agentic AI workloads' while the source says 'optimized for performance-per-dollar-per-watt leadership' with 'advanced memory innovations, including LPDDR.' Substance matches; wording differs. This is a recoverable correction.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No promotional language despite reporting a product announcement.
editor_notes.originality: No prior EPYC Venice articles in archive. The referenced AMD market share article (May 21) exists.
concerns: Verano description paraphrased inside quote marks — AMD press release wording differs from what the article quotes.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid semiconductor coverage with one paraphrased quote in quote marks. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
